description: Create your own custom data types to group related variables and model real-world entities.

slides.title: Grouping Data
slides.content:
# Beyond Arrays 📦

Arrays are great, but they can only hold one type of data (e.g., only `int`).

What if you want to represent a **Student**? You need:
- Name (`char array`)
- Age (`int`)
- GPA (`float`)

**Structures** allow you to group variables of **different types** under a single name.

slides.content: If `p` is a pointer to struct `Emp`, how do you access member `id`?

slides.content: `struct A {int i; char c;};` vs `union B {int i; char c;};` (Assuming int=4, char=1)

slides.title: Enumerations (enum)
Full Example: Employee Database
Summary
slides.content:
# Recap: Data Structures 🎓

You can now model complex data!

## Key Takeaways
- **Struct:** Groups different data types. Each member has its own memory.
- **Union:** Shares memory. Only one active member at a time.
- **Dot `.`**: For direct access `s.age`.
- **Arrow `->`**: For pointer access `ptr->age`.
- **Typedef:** Creates clean type aliases.
- **Enum:** Makes code readable with named constants.

Next up is **File Handling**, where we'll learn to save this data permanently!
